channel.title: NRIBeat — Daily Pulse for Indian Americans
channel.description: Immigration, AI, Cricket, Bollywood, and Layoffs — curated daily for the Indian-American community.

item.title: Aakhri Sawal: First Hindi Film with Indian Sign Language, May 8
item.description: Aakhri Sawal becomes the first Hindi film to release with Indian Sign Language accessibility on May 8, 2026. Starring Sanjay Dutt, this historical drama explores the RSS with serious storytelling. Streaming details on Netflix, Prime Video, and Hotstar US will be confirmed post-theatrical release.

item.title: Ayush Mhatre Out of IPL 2025: CSK Star's Hamstring Injury Explained
item.description: CSK's leading run-scorer Ayush Mhatre is out for 6-12 weeks due to a hamstring injury, significantly impacting the franchise's IPL 2025 campaign. This is a major blow to their title hopes during a critical phase of the season. US-based cricket fans can follow all updates via Disney+ Hotstar and NRIBeat.com.

item.title: Knight-Stokes Cup 2026: India Cricket's Youth Revolution Begins
item.description: The inaugural Knight-Stokes Cup 2026 just launched India's biggest youth cricket revolution, bringing 1,100+ teams from 820 state schools to compete at Lord's—breaking down barriers and democratizing access to elite cricket development. This tournament is reshaping India's talent pipeline and could produce future IPL 2025 stars and Test match legends. US-based Indian cricket fans can stream it on Sony LIV and YouTube with highlights available next-day for convenient viewing.

item.title: April 2026 Visa Bulletin: EB-2 India Final Action Date Holds Steady to July 15, 2014
item.description: April 2026 Visa Bulletin: EB-2 India Final Action Date holds steady to July 15, 2014; EB-3 India to November 15, 2013. Dates for Filing: EB-2 January 15, 2015, EB-3 January 15, 2015. No movement this month — monitor the next bulletin closely.

item.title: Google Cloud AI Agents: What It Means for Your Career in 2026
item.description: Google Cloud's move to agentic AI is forcing a fundamental reset in how enterprises build systems. For Indian engineers in the US, this means your skills are shifting from tool mastery to autonomous system design—and your salary is likely to follow. Start building with Google Cloud agents now to stay ahead of the curve.

item.title: Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai: New Bollywood Comedy 2025
item.description: Varun Dhawan stars in Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai, a new David Dhawan comedy arriving in 2025. The TIPS Films production will hit theaters first, then move to streaming platforms like Netflix or Prime Video in the US. Perfect for comedy lovers in the diaspora.

item.title: Cooper Connolly IPL 2025: Punjab Kings' Batting Maestro
item.description: Cooper Connolly's explosive IPL 2025 debut with Punjab Kings—where he's the franchise's leading run-getter—shows how new partnerships and mutual learning between batters can transform a team's fortunes. His chemistry with Arya has already yielded two victories, making this Australian's first season one to watch closely.

item.title: PBKS Bowling Attack IPL 2026: Multiple Guns, One Strategy
item.description: PBKS's IPL 2026 bowling attack is built on variety, not star power. With five different bowlers — pacer Jansen, spinner Chahal, youth sensation Arshdeep, and international options Bartlett and Vyshak — they can 'pull different triggers' at different stages. This strategic depth could be their biggest advantage as they chase the title.

item.title: Purple Cap Race Heats Up: Prince Yadav Enters Top 3 in IPL 2026
item.description: Prince Yadav has broken into the Purple Cap top three after Sunday's IPL 2026 double-header between KKR vs RR and PBKS vs LSG. The bowling race is heating up with multiple contenders pushing hard, and for US-based Indian cricket fans, Willow TV and JIO TV remain your best options for catching live action or highlights at convenient times.

item.title: Claude AI & ChatGPT Alternatives: What Changed in 2026
item.description: Claude Mythos challenges ChatGPT's dominance in developer tools, but the bigger story is AI security and governance becoming compliance requirements. Indian engineers who can architect safe, multi-model AI systems are commanding 15-20% salary premiums in 2026.

item.title: Tabu Buys ₹10 Crore Luxury Apartment in Mumbai 2026
item.description: Tabu has invested ₹10 crores in a luxury apartment at Godrej Skyshore in Mumbai's Versova locality. This move reflects the financial success of top Bollywood actors and their commitment to premium real estate investments in India's entertainment capital.

item.title: Shivaleeka Oberoi & Abhishek Pathak Welcome Baby Girl 2026
item.description: Bollywood actor Shivaleeka Oberoi and director Abhishek Pathak welcomed their first child, a baby girl, on April 19, 2026, during the auspicious Akshaya Tritiya. The couple announced the joyful news on social media with heartfelt gratitude to their loved ones and fans.

item.title: New Zealand Cricket 2026: Foxcroft's Brilliant 59 vs Bangladesh
item.description: New Zealand batter Dean Foxcroft scored a brilliant 59 off 58 balls in his second ODI against Bangladesh in Dhaka, proving that smart cricket beats flashy cricket. His unbeaten innings was the turning point that led New Zealand to victory in challenging subcontinental conditions. For Indian cricket fans in the US, this match highlights the depth of international cricket beyond India-centric series.

item.title: IPL 2026 Purple Cap: Kamboj Takes Lead, Klaasen Reclaims Orange Cap
item.description: IPL 2026 saw major shake-ups on Saturday: Kamboj claimed the Purple Cap as top wicket-taker with stellar bowling, while Heinrich Klaasen recaptured the Orange Cap as leading run-scorer with aggressive batting. If you're watching from the US, catch full match highlights on ESPN+ or Willow TV, with YouTube clips available by afternoon.

item.title: Coetzee Replaces Injured Payne at SRH in IPL 2026
item.description: SRH brings in Coetzee to replace injured David Payne for IPL 2026, marking the third overseas seamer replacement this season. The franchise is facing a major injury crisis but hopes for Pat Cummins' quick return to stabilize their bowling attack.

item.title: India Cricket Score: Rodrigues on T20I Loss to South Africa
item.description: India's T20I loss to South Africa exposed execution gaps in death overs, but Jemimah Rodrigues is determined to lead a comeback. The series is still very much alive—watch for India cricket highlights on Willow TV and catch the next match with NRIBeat's complete coverage.

item.title: New Zealand's Foxcroft Shines in Bangladesh ODI 2026
item.description: New Zealand's Dean Foxcroft proved he belongs in international cricket with a composed 59-run innings in his second ODI against Bangladesh in Dhaka. Young players demonstrating such adaptability in subcontinental conditions are building the next generation of international cricketers—comparable to talent emerging from the IPL 2025 franchise circuit. For US-based India cricket fans, Foxcroft's performance is a reminder that ODI cricket rewards tactical thinking and condition awareness as much as raw batting talent.

item.title: Vipul Amrutlal Shah Wins 16-Year Legal Battle Over London Dreams
item.description: Filmmaker Vipul Amrutlal Shah wins 16-year cheque bounce case tied to 2009 film London Dreams after court judgment April 15, 2026. This landmark victory strengthens protections for Bollywood creators and signals improved accountability in Hindi film financing—important context for diaspora audiences following new Bollywood movies and streaming releases.

item.title: AI Won't Replace Your Thinking—But Will Atrophy It [2026]
item.description: Dani Rodrik warns that AI's real threat isn't job replacement—it's cognitive atrophy. Indian engineers in the US who use AI thoughtfully (understanding why, not just copying) will protect and advance their careers. Those who outsource their thinking to AI will become interchangeable with a subscription.

item.title: Pati Patni Aur Woh Do 2026: Zee Studios & T-Series Team Up
item.description: Zee Studios and T-Series have partnered for the first time to release 'Pati Patni Aur Woh Do' in 2026, a highly-anticipated comedy sequel. The film will premiere theatrically before streaming on OTT platforms (likely ZEE5), making it essential viewing for Bollywood fans in the USA.
